name: Río Escuriza - El Campillo Trail
name_original: Río Escuriza - El Campillo — circular desde Gargallo - Norte Teruel (BTT10)
description: This moderate 16.9-mile (27.1 km) mountain biking trail in Maestrazgo Cultural Park offers granite formations and passes through charming vi
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Gargallo offer a diverse range of routes for all skill levels. The region features varied terrain, from challenging climbs to more moderate paths. Mountain bikers can explore different landscapes, providing a dynamic experience. Best mountain bike trails around Gargallo The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierra de Majalinos Route, a 38.1 miles (61.3 km) trail that takes 6 hours 10 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain, making it suitable for experienced riders. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Masías de Ejulve BTT 01 Trail, a moderate 28.1 miles (45.2 km) path. This route offers a balanced ride through varied landscapes. Local mountain bikers also love the Río Escuriza - El Campillo Trail, a 16.9 miles (27.1 km) trail leading through natural surroundings, often completed in about 2 hours 31 minutes. Mountain biking around Gargallo is defined by varied terrain, including moderate climbs and longer distances. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy to difficult. The routes in Gargallo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 19 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Gargallo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Gargallo?
A: There are over 7 mountain bike trails around Gargallo, offering a range of options for different skill levels. You can find routes from easy to difficult, ensuring there's something for every rider.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Gargallo?
A: While specific seasonal conditions for Gargallo are not detailed, mountain biking is generally best enjoyed during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be hot, and winter might bring colder conditions, depending on the specific elevation of the trails.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Gargallo?
A: Yes, Gargallo offers at least one easy mountain bike trail. These routes are perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride, often featuring less challenging terrain and elevation changes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike routes in Gargallo?
A: Mountain biking around Gargallo is characterized by varied terrain, including moderate climbs and longer distances. You'll encounter a mix of landscapes, from natural surroundings along trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999033798/rio-escuriza-el-campillo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Escuriza - El Campillo Trail&lt;/a&gt; to more challenging sections with significant elevation gain on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999030439/sierra-de-majalinos-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Majalinos Route&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Gargallo?
A: Yes, there are several circular mountain bike routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996657655/ruta-de-la-muela-por-el-monasterio-del-olivar-circular-desde-estercuel-btt05" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de la Muela por el Monasterio del Olivar&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option starting from Estercuel, offering a great way to explore the area and return to your starting point.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Gargallo?
A: The mountain bike routes in Gargallo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Riders often praise the varied terrain and the dynamic experience offered by the trails, which cater to different ability levels.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions along the mountain bike trails in Gargallo?
A: Yes, several interesting landmarks and settlements can be found near the mountain bike trails. You might pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5361244" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Santa María del Olivar&lt;/a&gt;, or through charming settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5361246" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estercuel&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5290366" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ejulve&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1637345" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Majalinos&lt;/a&gt; is also a significant point of interest.

Q: Is there parking available for mountain bikers in Gargallo?
A: While specific parking locations are not detailed, trailheads for popular routes like those near Estercuel or Ejulve typically offer parking facilities for visitors. It's advisable to check the individual route descriptions on komoot for precise parking information.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Gargallo?
A: Yes, Gargallo offers options suitable for families. The easier trails are generally more appropriate for family outings, providing a less strenuous experience. Look for routes categorized as 'easy' for a more relaxed ride with children.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Gargallo?
A: Many outdoor trails in the region are dog-friendly, but it's always best to check local regulations for specific trails or protected areas. Ensure your dog is well-behaved, kept on a leash where required, and that you carry waste bags to maintain the cleanliness of the trails.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike trail in Gargallo?
A: The longest mountain bike trail listed is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999030439/sierra-de-majalinos-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Majalinos Route&lt;/a&gt;, which spans approximately 38.1 miles (61.3 km). This route is classified as difficult and involves significant elevation gain, making it suitable for experienced riders.

Q: Are there any bike shops or rental services in Gargallo?
A: While specific bike shops or rental services in Gargallo are not detailed, larger towns or tourist centers in the surrounding area may offer these facilities. It's recommended to check local directories or inquire with accommodation providers before your visit.
tips: We are in the town of Gargallo, located at the foot of Muela Alta. Another of the beautiful towns in Teruel where we highlight the Church of Nuestra Señora de la Piedad, the Hermitage of San Blas or the Nacimiento del Río Escuriza.

We are in the beautiful town of Ejulve, located in the Andorra-Sierra de Arcos region. We, adventurers, highlight this town for being the starting point of The Silent Route tourist route that covers several of the most outstanding places in the region by bicycle. Throughout the municipality of Ejulve is the Órganos de Montoro Natural Monument, the Cueva del Recuenco, the Sierra de Majalinos or the Masías de Ejulve, which also have their own BTT route.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Río Escuriza - El Campillo Trail for mountain biking?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as moderate for mountain biking. While it doesn't have extreme technical sections, its length of approximately 27 kilometers and an elevation gain of nearly 500 meters mean it requires a good level of fitness to complete comfortably.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route features varied terrain, typical of the region. You can expect sections of tamped gravel and potentially some asphalt, similar to the nearby Via Verde de la Jara. Be prepared for natural surfaces that may include granite and slate formations, and occasionally roots and stones, adding to the mountain biking experience.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for the Río Escuriza - El Campillo Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route, making access convenient from several points. A common starting point is near Gargallo. Look for designated parking areas within or on the outskirts of villages like Gargallo Village or Ejulve Village, which are both along the route and offer good access points.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike the Río Escuriza - El Campillo Trail?
faqs.answer: The ideal seasons for mountain biking this trail are spring and fall. In spring, the landscape comes alive with wildflowers, including the 'Jara' (rock rose), offering picturesque views. Fall provides pleasant temperatures and beautiful autumn colors. Summers can be very hot in this region, making riding less comfortable, especially during midday.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Río Escuriza - El Campillo Trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails in this region. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through villages or near livestock, and to ensure you clean up after them. Respect local regulations and other trail users.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need special permits or to pay entrance fees to mountain bike the Río Escuriza - El Campillo Trail. It is a public access route. However, as the trail is located within the Maestrazgo Cultural Park, please adhere to all park regulations regarding environmental protection and responsible recreation.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or villages will I encounter along the route?
faqs.answer: The circular route passes through charming local villages. You'll ride near Gargallo Village and Ejulve Village, offering glimpses into local life and potential spots for a break. The natural scenery includes granite and slate formations, and depending on the section, views of the Sierra de Altamira.

faqs.question: Does the Río Escuriza - El Campillo Trail intersect with other mountain biking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, while the 'Río Escuriza - El Campillo' path makes up 99% of this specific komoot Tour, the route also briefly intersects with sections of other official trails. You might find yourself on parts of the 'Ejulve - Gargallo - Las Calderas' trail for about 60% of its length, and very small portions of 'Sierra de Majalinos' and 'Subida a Majalinos'.

faqs.question: What are the regulations for mountain biking within the Maestrazgo Cultural Park?
faqs.answer: As the Río Escuriza - El Campillo Trail is situated within the Maestrazgo Cultural Park, riders are expected to follow general park regulations. This includes staying on marked trails, respecting wildlife and natural habitats, not littering, and being mindful of other visitors. There are no specific permits required for mountain biking, but responsible behavior is paramount to preserve the park's natural and cultural heritage.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the villages along and near the trail, such as Gargallo Village and Ejulve Village, offer local amenities. You can find small cafes or pubs for refreshments and traditional Spanish meals. For accommodation, there are often rural guesthouses or small hotels in these villages, some of which may cater to cyclists with bike storage facilities.

faqs.question: Is the Río Escuriza - El Campillo Trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty rating, length of 27 km, and nearly 500m of elevation gain, this specific trail might be challenging for very young children or those new to mountain biking. However, families with older, experienced, and fit children who are comfortable with moderate climbs and varied terrain could enjoy it. For an easier family ride, consider shorter, flatter sections of nearby routes or the Via Verde de la Jara, which is generally considered easy.
